rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending scarcity research on informational goods and contrasting its mechanism (risk of underprocessing) with value-from-limited-supply accounts, which makes a scarcity-mechanism paper like the 2015 JMR article plausibly theory-relevant rather than incidental. The cited paper note centers on scarcity-driven arousal and choice polarization, and the DOI match confirms it is directly in the reference list.
